summary.caption_backend: Gemini Web subscription via Antigravity CLI

pipeline_stages.name: Caption Scope
pipeline_stages.detail: Whole Piece default with two explicit comparison conditions

pipeline_stages.name: Evidence Projection
pipeline_stages.detail: Scope-specific key, melody, rhythm, texture, and velocity evidence

pipeline_stages.name: LLM-Based Caption Filtering
pipeline_stages.detail: Independent semantic claim filtering not yet run

condition_matrix.input: Scope-aware general metadata; no file attachment
Whole-piece or cropped MIDI according to scope

caveats: All three cases use the same actual POP909 Train piece so that caption scope is the only intended comparison axis.
Whole Piece is training-eligible; the fixed-window region and physical crop remain diagnostic until a phrase or section selector is validated.
POP909 key_audio annotations are audio-derived dataset annotations, not calibrated ground truth.
No full-piece audio was rendered in A.1.2; the only WAV is the deterministic legacy-crop render.

cases.title: Whole Piece Caption
cases.prompt:
You are producing a natural English music caption for the explicitly scoped symbolic music target below.

This is a generation-caption task. Describe only the current musical state represented by the supplied evidence.

Caption scope:
Summarize the entire piece. Do not turn a whole-piece aggregate into a local event, and do not invent section-level changes that are absent from supported evidence.

Evidence policy:
- Use only fields under `metadata.supported_evidence` whose status is `supported`.
- Treat `metadata.context_evidence` as interpretation context, not as direct caption claims.
- Treat `metadata.scopes` and `metadata.caption_scope` as targeting instructions, not as caption evidence. Do not mention the target length, bars, or timestamps unless a corresponding field exists under `metadata.supported_evidence`.
- Treat `metadata.uncertain_or_unknown_evidence` as blocked attributes.
- Preserve lexical strength: a key with `assertion_level: centered` must be described as centered around that key, not definitively in that key.
- Use an overall melodic direction only when `overall_direction.value` is explicitly supplied for the caption target.
- An opening or ending pitch sequence may be named only from the corresponding exact sequence field.
- Treat MIDI velocity as symbolic performance evidence, not acoustic loudness.
- Do not convert absolute velocity values into qualitative terms such as high, low, loud, soft, strong, or weak. Use only an explicitly supplied velocity direction, or omit dynamics.
- A median tempo is a summary statistic, not a constant tempo. Describe it as a median or approximate tempo rather than saying the entire piece is at that tempo.
- Do not infer genre, emotion, timbre, performer identity, performance quality, or acoustic properties.
- Do not provide hidden chain-of-thought. `evidence_trace` must contain only short, externally verifiable claim-to-field links.

Writing policy:
- Prioritize two to four musically useful properties rather than enumerating every number.
- Prefer natural musical descriptions over database-like field listings.
- Across the entire response, include at most one pitch sequence and no more than four pitch-name tokens in total.
- Keep the short caption to one concise sentence and the detailed caption to one or two sentences.

Return exactly one raw JSON object and no Markdown. It must match this contract:
{
  "schema_version": "sori_music_caption_response_v0.1",
  "job_id": "caption_597a0534ccdb",
  "caption_short": "<one concise sentence>",
  "caption_detailed": "<one or two evidence-grounded sentences>",
  "evidence_trace": [
    {
      "claim": "<short verifiable claim>",
      "evidence_paths": [
        "/metadata/supported_evidence/melody_summary"
      ],
      "certainty": "supported"
    }
  ],
  "uncertain_attributes": [],
  "unsupported_claims": []
}

Replace angle-bracketed placeholder strings with content. Keep `schema_version`, `job_id`, field names, and `certainty` exactly as shown. Every evidence path must identify a field actually used for its claim and must start with `/metadata/supported_evidence/`.

Job ID:

response.caption_detailed: Played at a median tempo of 71 BPM, this 86-bar piece contains three active parts centered around D minor. The melody features 356 notes spanning a range of 21 semitones.
response.caption_short: This three-part piece is centered around D minor with a median tempo of 71 BPM.

evidence_trace.claim: The piece is centered around the estimated tonal center.
The piece contains melody, bridge, and piano parts.
The piece has a median tempo of 71 BPM.
The melody spans a pitch range of 21 semitones.

cases.researcher_audit: This is the A.1.2 training default: the caption and metadata summarize the entire piece. Uncalibrated whole-song contour and exact long note sequences are excluded.

media.render_note: Original full-piece POP909 MIDI; audio not rendered in A.1.2

cases.title: Region In Full Context Caption

overall_direction.definition: least-squares pitch slope with endpoint agreement

You are producing a natural English music caption for the explicitly scoped symbolic music target below.

This is a generation-caption task. Describe only the current musical state represented by the supplied evidence.

Caption scope:
Describe only metadata.scopes.caption_span. The whole piece is analysis context; do not present context_evidence as a property of the target span unless an explicit supported relation is supplied.

Evidence policy:
- Use only fields under `metadata.supported_evidence` whose status is `supported`.
- Treat `metadata.context_evidence` as interpretation context, not as direct caption claims.
- Treat `metadata.scopes` and `metadata.caption_scope` as targeting instructions, not as caption evidence. Do not mention the target length, bars, or timestamps unless a corresponding field exists under `metadata.supported_evidence`.
- Treat `metadata.uncertain_or_unknown_evidence` as blocked attributes.
- Preserve lexical strength: a key with `assertion_level: centered` must be described as centered around that key, not definitively in that key.
- Use an overall melodic direction only when `overall_direction.value` is explicitly supplied for the caption target.
- An opening or ending pitch sequence may be named only from the corresponding exact sequence field.
- Treat MIDI velocity as symbolic performance evidence, not acoustic loudness.
- Do not convert absolute velocity values into qualitative terms such as high, low, loud, soft, strong, or weak. Use only an explicitly supplied velocity direction, or omit dynamics.
- A median tempo is a summary statistic, not a constant tempo. Describe it as a median or approximate tempo rather than saying the entire piece is at that tempo.
- Do not infer genre, emotion, timbre, performer identity, performance quality, or acoustic properties.
- Do not provide hidden chain-of-thought. `evidence_trace` must contain only short, externally verifiable claim-to-field links.

Writing policy:
- Prioritize two to four musically useful properties rather than enumerating every number.
- Prefer natural musical descriptions over database-like field listings.
- Across the entire response, include at most one pitch sequence and no more than four pitch-name tokens in total.
- Keep the short caption to one concise sentence and the detailed caption to one or two sentences.

Return exactly one raw JSON object and no Markdown. It must match this contract:

}

response.caption_detailed: Centered around D minor, the passage presents an ascending melody with a median inter-onset interval of 0.5 beats. The three-part texture is performed with mixed velocity changes.
response.caption_short: Centered around D minor, the passage features an ascending melody played with mixed velocity changes in a three-part texture.

evidence_trace.claim: The music is centered around D minor
The rhythm features a median inter-onset interval of 0.5 beats
The passage has a three-part texture

cases.researcher_audit: The caption targets one region while the complete MIDI remains available as context. This diagnostic case is blocked from training export until a phrase or section selector is validated.

media.render_note: Original full-piece POP909 MIDI; audio not rendered in A.1.2

cases.title: Cropped Region Legacy Caption

overall_direction.definition: least-squares pitch slope with endpoint agreement

cases.prompt:
You are producing a natural English music caption for the explicitly scoped symbolic music target below.

This is a generation-caption task. Describe only the current musical state represented by the supplied evidence.

Caption scope:
Describe only the supplied cropped region. This condition is a legacy comparison baseline.

Evidence policy:
- Use only fields under `metadata.supported_evidence` whose status is `supported`.
- Treat `metadata.context_evidence` as interpretation context, not as direct caption claims.
- Treat `metadata.scopes` and `metadata.caption_scope` as targeting instructions, not as caption evidence. Do not mention the target length, bars, or timestamps unless a corresponding field exists under `metadata.supported_evidence`.
- Treat `metadata.uncertain_or_unknown_evidence` as blocked attributes.
- Preserve lexical strength: a key with `assertion_level: centered` must be described as centered around that key, not definitively in that key.
- Use an overall melodic direction only when `overall_direction.value` is explicitly supplied for the caption target.
- An opening or ending pitch sequence may be named only from the corresponding exact sequence field.
- Treat MIDI velocity as symbolic performance evidence, not acoustic loudness.
- Do not convert absolute velocity values into qualitative terms such as high, low, loud, soft, strong, or weak. Use only an explicitly supplied velocity direction, or omit dynamics.
- A median tempo is a summary statistic, not a constant tempo. Describe it as a median or approximate tempo rather than saying the entire piece is at that tempo.
- Do not infer genre, emotion, timbre, performer identity, performance quality, or acoustic properties.
- Do not provide hidden chain-of-thought. `evidence_trace` must contain only short, externally verifiable claim-to-field links.

Writing policy:
- Prioritize two to four musically useful properties rather than enumerating every number.
- Prefer natural musical descriptions over database-like field listings.
- Across the entire response, include at most one pitch sequence and no more than four pitch-name tokens in total.
- Keep the short caption to one concise sentence and the detailed caption to one or two sentences.

Return exactly one raw JSON object and no Markdown. It must match this contract:
{
  "schema_version": "sori_music_caption_response_v0.1",
  "job_id": "caption_8123b2a86b5c",
  "caption_short": "<one concise sentence>",
  "caption_detailed": "<one or two evidence-grounded sentences>",
  "evidence_trace": [
    {
      "claim": "<short verifiable claim>",
      "evidence_paths": [
        "/metadata/supported_evidence/dynamics_summary"
      ],
      "certainty": "supported"
    }
  ],
  "uncertain_attributes": [],
  "unsupported_claims": []
}

Replace angle-bracketed placeholder strings with content. Keep `schema_version`, `job_id`, field names, and `certainty` exactly as shown. Every evidence path must identify a field actually used for its claim and must start with `/metadata/supported_evidence/`.

response.caption_detailed: The music is centered around D minor, featuring three active roles and a mean polyphony of approximately 5.3 voices. The twenty-five note melody progresses in an ascending direction with a dominant half-beat inter-onset interval and mixed MIDI note-on velocities that have a median of 107.5.
response.caption_short: Centered around D minor, this excerpt features an ascending melody within a texture of three active roles.

evidence_trace.claim: The music is centered around D minor.
The texture features three active roles and a mean polyphony of approximately 5.3 voices.
The melody contains twenty-five notes and has an ascending direction.
The dominant inter-onset interval is a half beat.
The MIDI velocities have a mixed direction and a median velocity of 107.5.

cases.researcher_audit: This physically cropped input preserves the A.1.1 comparison condition. It is an ablation baseline rather than the caption-pretraining default.
